response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer but their core expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the JVM stack this role requires. Java appears only briefly (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tool) and Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, and Hibernate are entirely absent from the resume. The National Instruments LabWindows/CVI work is the only IDE-internals signal, but it is over two decades old and C/C++ based. Without demonstrated Java/Kotlin depth or Spring ecosystem experience, the fit for an enterprise JVM IDE tooling role is weak.
strengths: Long career across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Some IDE/tooling exposure via National Instruments LabWindows/CVI work in C/C++
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, workshops, OSS test tooling)
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only superficial Java exposure; deep JVM expertise not evidenced
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise framework experience listed
Primary stack (TypeScript/Node, DLang) is misaligned with the role's enterprise JVM focus

key_factors.factor: No Kotlin and only shallow Java background
Some historical IDE/language-tooling exposure (LabWindows/CVI, test runners)
